children.heading: .—Acceptance of Assignment of Foreign Naval Personnel
children.label: a .—Acceptance of Assignment of Foreign Naval Personnel
children.text: In order to facilitate the development, standardization, and interoperability of submarine vessel safety and rescue systems and procedures, the Secretary of the Navy may conduct a program beginning on or after the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2024 under which members of the naval service of any of the member nations of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization may be assigned to United States commands to work on such systems and procedures.

children.heading: .—Reciprocity Not Required
children.label: b .—Reciprocity Not Required
children.text: The authority under subsection (a) is not an exchange program. Reciprocal assignments of members of the Navy to the naval service of a foreign country is not a condition for the exercise of such authority.

children.label: E
children.text: Cost of language or other training.

children.label: A
children.text: The cost of temporary duty directed by the Secretary of the Navy or an officer of the Navy authorized to do so.

children.label: B
children.text: The cost of training programs conducted to familiarize, orient, or certify members of foreign naval services regarding unique aspects of their assignments.

children.label: C
children.text: Costs incident to the use of the facilities of the Navy in the performance of assigned duties.

children.heading: .—Relationship to Other Authority
children.label: d .—Relationship to Other Authority
children.text: The provisions of this section shall apply in the exercise of any authority of the Secretary of the Navy to enter into an agreement with the government of a foreign country, subject to the concurrence of the Secretary of State, to provide for the assignment of members of the naval service of the foreign country to a Navy submarine safety program. The Secretary of the Navy may prescribe regulations for the application of this section in the exercise of such authority.
